summary.intent: Automate the `direnv allow` step in the worktree setup script so developers don't have to run it manually after setup.
summary.outcome: Added `direnv allow` to `.superset/setup.sh` immediately after the `.envrc` file is copied into the worktree, then committed and pushed the change.

repo: The superset setup script lives at .superset/setup.sh and handles worktree initialization including mise trust, beads attachment, and .envrc copying.
Worktrees are created under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name> with beads pointing to /Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/.beads.

code.finding: direnv allow must be called after .envrc is copied into the worktree to avoid requiring a manual approval step.

workflow: A stash/pull-rebase/pop sequence was needed before committing, suggesting the remote had diverged from local.

friction: direnv blocks .envrc by default and requires manual `direnv allow` after each new worktree setup — the script did not handle this automatically.
Local branch was behind remote, requiring a stash + pull --rebase + pop before the commit could land cleanly.
